title: Diabetic kidney disease review

investigations.text: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.
investigations.rationale: Supports renal, ACR, blood-pressure and diabetes review domains for diabetic kidney disease review; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Specifies hypoglycaemia awareness, home glucose review, BP and BMI, renal testing, urine ACR, HbA1c, cholesterol, podiatry, and retinal review.

follow_up.text: Supports ACR, PCR, urinalysis, quantitative confirmation, and interpretation factors including haematuria, menstruation, exercise, and infection.

follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports ACR, PCR, urinalysis, quantitative confirmation, and interpretation factors including haematuria, menstruation, exercise, and infection.

exact_section.heading: Evaluation of albuminuria

follow_up.text: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Diabetes type and duration, glycaemia, GFR and ACR trends, blood pressure, medicines, adherence, complications, diagnosis, treatment and follow-up remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for diabetic kidney disease review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
